axis_responsibility.Agent: Owns Agent Object adapter_refs and optional preferred_adapter_ref capability preference; it does not choose Movement, target, success, or quality.
axis_responsibility.Brick: Owns preset step declarations, Building Plan step fields, required return shapes, fleet graph shape, reroute-budget declaration, and closure policy as declared work composition.
axis_responsibility.Evidence: Checker cases, profile output, and this design return are support evidence only.
axis_responsibility.Link: Owns gate sufficiency, Movement, target carrying, fan-in frontier observation, and reroute budget semantics; no Link vocabulary change is proposed.
axis_responsibility.Support: Materializes declared presets, resolves declared/provider preferences mechanically, validates shape, records evidence, and walks declared plans without authoring route or Movement.

returned.design_summary: Implement the provider ladder only in Builder/materializer and Agent-resource validation surfaces: step declaration wins, then optional Agent Object preferred_adapter_ref, then the caller/COO-declared building adapter default. selected_model_ref remains step declaration then building default/model:default. Fleet presets should be ordinary graph presets with declared fan-out/fan-in topology, declared reroute budgets, declared closure transition policy, strict/fan-in gate concepts, and at most one explicit claude-local lens. No Link vocabulary, scheduler, parallel runtime, or engine walk-order change is needed.
edge_cases: A building-level concrete codex model plus a lane preference to claude-local should reject unless the step declares a provider-matched model or uses model:default.

adapter:gemini-local is an admitted adapter vocabulary item but current Agent Objects observed here do not declare it, so a fleet step selecting it should reject until an Agent Object declares that capability.

Current compact graph materializer derives node ids from step_template_ref, so a fleet preset with duplicate step_template_ref rows would collide unless a separate node-id/lens-id authoring extension is admitted.

The review Brick is a leader-lane qa-lead shape; reviewer/read-only fleet branches should use inspect, code-attack-qa, axis-attack-qa, or evidence-integrity unless leader review is intentionally declared.
A step-only adapter declaration without a building-level selected_adapter_ref would be a policy change from the current fail-closed materializer and should remain rejected unless separately admitted.
invariants: If a preset step declares selected_adapter_ref, that exact adapter must appear in the rendered declared plan step.
A selected per-step adapter must be listed in the resolved Agent Object adapter_refs, or the path must reject loudly.
preferred_adapter_ref is optional, single-valued, and valid only when it is already in the same Agent Object adapter_refs.
support must not default selected_adapter_ref; building-level default remains a caller/COO declaration.
model:default remains a sentinel that lets the selected adapter use its own default model; concrete model refs must match the selected adapter provider.
Fleet presets may use gate_concept_profile labels already admitted, but fan-in-wait-all remains graph/frontier observation, not a new GateFact literal.
Any inspection/fan-in fleet preset that can reroute back must declare node_reroute_budgets and closure_transition_target_policy with human/preset provenance.
Only one claude-local review lens may be admitted in the new fleet examples unless a later provider policy changes.
not_proven: No implementation was performed in this read-only pass.
Full gate exit 0 is not proven in this sandbox because checker profiles hit No usable temporary directory found.
Provider availability, provider quality, model availability, and mixed-provider runtime behavior are not proven.
Codex native sync, Claude native sync, and MCP projection behavior are not proven.
Future fleet semantic correctness is not proven.
No Claude/Gemini read-only review was run for this design return.
No source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, or Movement authority is claimed.

Catalog/profile coverage already checks preset file presence, preset frontmatter, step refs, brick spec refs, gate concept tokens, target words, graph fan-out/fan-in groups, gate placement, route-policy provenance, and all-preset materialization, but not per-step provider selection or fleet provider policy

proposed_changes: Add optional preferred_adapter_ref to the Agent Object admitted key set and validate it as one non-empty string present in that object's adapter_refs; reject unknown, retired, or out-of-set preferences loudly.

Add a provider-selection helper for materialization that receives the resolved Agent Object and computes selected_adapter_ref by step selected_adapter_ref > preferred_adapter_ref > intent selected_adapter_ref; keep intent selected_adapter_ref required so support still does not invent an adapter default.

Stamp step-level selected_adapter_ref whenever the step declaration or Agent preference differs from the building default; preserve plan-level selected_adapter_ref as the fallback for undeclared steps.

Carry selected_model_ref from step declaration when present, otherwise building selected_model_ref/model:default; do not rewrite concrete provider models when adapter changes, and let provider mismatch reject loudly.
Validate the resolved per-step adapter against the resolved Agent Object adapter_refs in the Builder/render path, while preserving the runtime guard as the final admission check.

Copy provider selections through both linear preset expansion (_materializer_declared_step) and graph preset expansion (_materializer_graph_node/compose_building) so graph fleets retain per-step mixed providers.

Add recon-fleet and review-fleet presets as graph presets with fan-in-wait-all plus strict-evidence, declared node_reroute_budgets, and closure_transition_target_policy; use codex-local as the caller/COO-declared default and declare at most one claude-local lens directly on a preset step.

Extend checker cases to assert per-step adapter stamping, mutated materializer ignoring step declaration RED, per-step adapter not in lane Agent Object RED, preferred_adapter_ref outside adapter_refs RED, fleet preset catalog admission, C1 repeat-lane policy, and at-most-one claude-local fleet lens.

relevant_current_structure.agent_resources: support/connection/agent_resources.py owns Agent Object key admission and adapter_refs validation.
relevant_current_structure.builder_materializer: support/operator/composition.py materializes compact chain presets and graph presets; it currently requires intent selected_adapter_ref and passes one plan-level adapter/model default.
relevant_current_structure.declared_step_renderer: support/operator/plan_rendering.py resolves step templates and Agent Objects, accepts step selected_adapter_ref/selected_model_ref, and renders selected fields onto steps.
relevant_current_structure.engine_validation: support/operator/plan_validation.py and support/operator/plan_graph.py already carry step-level adapter/model values to run preparation.
relevant_current_structure.model_projection: support/connection/agent_adapter.py owns selected_model_ref provider matching and CLI model projection.
relevant_current_structure.preset_catalog: brick/templates/presets/*.md frontmatter is loaded whole into the chain preset registry; catalog checker validates current preset shape but not provider-selection policy.
relevant_current_structure.runtime_guard: support/operator/run.py checks selected_adapter_ref against prepared.agent_object.adapter_refs before creating AgentAdapterRequest.

returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
unchanged_surfaces: No link/ edits, no new Link Movement literal, no new GateFact shape, and no gate authority move.
No scheduler, queue, retry runtime, or parallel provider runtime opens; sequential engine walking remains acceptable.
No provider credentials, setup tokens, session bodies, or raw provider state are stored.
No change to effective_write law: Brick write_scope NEED plus Agent tool policy plus observed-write adapter plus write observation remains required.
Agent Object remains Agent-axis resource meaning; selected_adapter_ref/selected_model_ref remain Building Plan step selection data, not Agent identity.
Checker/profile output remains support evidence only, not source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, or Movement authority.
